title: 2026 Florida U.S. Senate special election
description: This is a special election for Florida’s U.S. Senate seat to fill the remainder of Marco Rubio’s term after his departure from the Senate. The winner will serve the final two years of the term, making the race relevant to Senate control and Florida’s federal representation. Florida has trended Republican in recent federal elections, but recent polling and campaign activity suggest the general election could be more competitive than in past cycles. The main contrast is between Republican incumbent Ashley Moody, who was appointed to the seat, and Democrat Alex Vindman, a retired Army officer and former National Security Council official.

candidates.name: Ashley Moody
candidates.summary: Ashley Moody is Florida’s appointed U.S. senator and the Republican candidate in the special election. She previously served as Florida attorney general and earlier worked as a judge and prosecutor. Her public messaging emphasizes law enforcement, border security, conservative policy priorities, and support for President Trump’s agenda.

career_history.title: United States Senator
career_history.description: Appointed to fill Florida’s Senate vacancy and serving in the seat up for special election.

career_history.title: Attorney General
career_history.description: Served as Florida’s attorney general before joining the Senate.

voting_record.bill_name: Confirmation of Marco Rubio as Secretary of State
PN 25-19 - Nomination of Allison Hooker to be an Under Secretary of State (Political Affairs)
PN 26-25 - Nomination of Matthew Kozma to be Under Secretary for Intelligence and Analysis, Department of Homeland Security

Healthcare.stance: Moody’s public record emphasizes health-care fraud enforcement and opposition to pandemic-era mandates. In the Senate, she has also voted against extending ACA premium subsidies, indicating a conservative approach to federal health-care policy.

sources.title: Senator Moody Targets Health Care Fraudsters, Pushes Tougher Penalties
Ashley Moody, Rick Scott vote to kill extension of health care subsidies

Economy.stance: Moody’s campaign says she supports policies aimed at economic prosperity, and her Senate messaging emphasizes lowering costs through housing and tax-related measures. Her public materials also stress cutting red tape and supporting working families.

sources.title: Ashley Moody for U.S. Senate
Ashley Moody Delivers on Affordable Housing

Climate/Energy.stance: Moody has emphasized support for Florida agriculture and resilience to extreme weather, including backing research into technologies that can lower costs, improve efficiency, and help farmers adapt to droughts, freezes, and hurricanes. She has also framed her energy posture around energy dominance and domestic energy production.

sources.title: NEWS RELEASE: Senator Moody, Colleagues, Introduce Bipartisan Legislation to Strengthen Florida Agriculture by Supporting New Farming Technology to Lower Costs and Increase Efficiency
We've got to bring back energy dominance, says Sen. Ashley Moody

Reproductive Rights.stance: Moody is publicly anti-abortion and has worked to block abortion-rights measures. She asked the Florida Supreme Court to keep a proposed abortion-rights amendment off the ballot and has backed legislation described as pro-life.

sources.title: Ashley Moody makes it official, tells court abortion that rights amendment should be blocked
NEWS RELEASE: Senator Moody Fights to Protect Babies Born After Botched Abortions
Senator Moody Supports Life, Sponsors Pregnant Students' Rights Act

Immigration.stance: Moody has taken a hard-line immigration stance, backing President Trump’s immigration agenda and introducing legislation to speed the removal of certain criminal noncitizens. Her Senate messaging also emphasizes border security and enforcement.

sources.title: Senator Ashley Moody Leads Efforts to Codify President Trump’s Immigration Priorities, Introduces Expedited Removal of Criminal Aliens Act
Ashley Moody for U.S. Senate
Alex Vindman, key figure in Trump's first impeachment, enters Florida Senate race

Guns & Safety.stance: Moody has framed herself as a defender of gun rights and opposed federal gun-control measures, including a Biden administration rule expanding background checks at gun shows. She has said such measures amount to federal overreach and burden law-abiding gun owners.

sources.title: Ashley Moody among 26 Republican AGs suing to block Joe Biden rule requiring background checks at gun shows
Sen. Ashley Moody on her legislative goals for this year and more

Foreign Policy.stance: Moody’s public materials emphasize support for President Trump’s agenda and American sovereignty, but they do not lay out a detailed foreign-policy platform. Her available Senate messaging is more focused on immigration, law enforcement, and domestic priorities than on specific foreign-policy proposals.

sources.title: Ashley Moody for U.S. Senate
Senator Ashley Moody

Social Justice.stance: Moody’s public record and messaging emphasize law enforcement, tougher criminal enforcement, and border/security priorities rather than social-justice reforms. The sources reviewed did not show a detailed public platform on broader civil-rights or social-justice policy changes.

sources.title: Sen. Ashley Moody on her legislative goals for this year and more
Press Releases Archive - Ashley Moody - Senate.gov

Education.stance: Moody has publicly backed school choice and educational freedom, saying student needs, not zip codes, should determine opportunities. She has also promoted Florida’s school-choice model nationally.

sources.title: VIDEO RELEASE: As Co-Chair of National School Choice Week, Senator Moody Promotes Educational Freedom in America, Touts Success in Florida’s Model
VIDEO RELEASE: Senator Moody Works to Bring Florida’s Educational Success to All Americans During National School Choice Week
Ashley Moody for U.S. Senate

Tech & AI.stance: Moody has called for Congress to investigate AI’s harms, especially its use in child exploitation and deepfakes, and has supported stronger restrictions on AI-generated abuse material. She has also backed AI use in agriculture and has discussed holding AI companies accountable for harmful outputs.

sources.title: Attorney General Moody Calls on Congress to Investigate AI and Child Exploitation
NEWS RELEASE: Senator Moody, Colleagues, Introduce Bipartisan Legislation to Strengthen Florida Agriculture by Supporting New Farming Technology to Lower Costs and Increase Efficiency
Senator Ashley Moody Joins Colleagues in Supporting the Kids Online Safety Act to Protect Kids Online

Election Reform.stance: Moody’s public record reviewed here does not show a detailed election-reform platform. Her campaign messaging instead focuses on election integrity, law and order, and opposition to corruption.

sources.title: Ashley Moody for U.S. Senate
About - Senator Moody

Local Issues.stance: Moody’s campaign and Senate messaging highlight Florida-specific concerns such as property insurance, disaster recovery, housing affordability, and support for Florida agriculture. She also frames her work around protecting Floridians from crime, border-related spillovers, and consumer fraud.

sources.title: About - Senator Moody
Ashley Moody for U.S. Senate
NEWS RELEASE: Senator Moody, Colleagues, Introduce Bipartisan Legislation to Strengthen Florida Agriculture by Supporting New Farming Technology to Lower Costs and Increase Efficiency

candidates.name: Alex Vindman
candidates.summary: Alex Vindman is a retired U.S. Army lieutenant colonel and former National Security Council official running as a Democrat in Florida’s Senate special election. He became nationally known for his role in the Trump impeachment inquiry and is campaigning on public service, anti-corruption themes, and affordability. His background is in the military and foreign policy rather than elected office.

summary_sources.title: Alex Vindman for Florida - Alex Vindman for Florida
Alex Vindman, key figure in Trump's first impeachment, enters Florida Senate race

career_history.title: Retired U.S. Army Lieutenant Colonel
career_history.description: Served in the Army for 21 years, including combat service and foreign-policy assignments.

career_history.title: Director for European Affairs
career_history.description: Served on the National Security Council and became a key witness in the Trump impeachment inquiry.

career_history.title: Senior Fellow / Scholar
career_history.organization: Harvard University and related policy institutions
career_history.description: Worked in academic and policy roles after leaving government service.

education.field: Russian, Eastern European, and Eurasian Studies

Healthcare.stance: Vindman says health care premiums are too high and that Florida families need relief from rising costs. His campaign materials do not provide a detailed health-care policy agenda, but his messaging centers on affordability and access.

sources.title: Alex Vindman for Florida - Alex Vindman for Florida
Alex Vindman, key figure in Trump's first impeachment, enters Florida Senate race

Economy.stance: Vindman says working families are paying the price from higher costs and rents, and that the economy feels rigged against them. He says he is running to lower costs, take on corporate greed, and make life more affordable for Floridians.

sources.title: Alex Vindman for Florida
Eugene Vindman for Congress video post supporting Alex Vindman

Climate/Energy.stance: Vindman’s public campaign materials reviewed here do not lay out a detailed climate platform. His broader messaging emphasizes affordability, infrastructure, and disaster-related concerns in Florida, but a specific climate or energy agenda was not clearly stated in the sources found.

sources.title: Alex Vindman for Florida - Alex Vindman for Florida
Alex Vindman, key figure in Trump's first impeachment, enters Florida Senate race

Reproductive Rights.stance: Vindman has not published a detailed abortion platform in the sources reviewed, but as a Democratic candidate he is generally aligned with protecting abortion access. This is an inference rather than a directly quoted candidate position.

sources.title: Alex Vindman, key figure in Trump's first impeachment, enters Florida Senate race
Whistleblower Alexander Vindman to seek Florida Democratic nomination for Senate

Immigration.stance: Vindman criticized Trump’s immigration crackdown, calling federal immigration agents “thug militias” in his campaign announcement video. His campaign framing suggests opposition to aggressive enforcement tactics, but the available sources do not provide a detailed immigration policy platform.

sources.title: Alex Vindman, key figure in Trump's first impeachment, enters Florida Senate race
Alex Vindman for Florida - Alex Vindman for Florida

Guns & Safety.stance: Vindman supports gun-safety measures and says he backs commonsense policies to get illegal guns off the streets and out of the hands of criminals. In a February 2026 statement, he said expanding nonlethal options for law enforcement strengthens public safety and helps protect lives.

sources.title: Vindman Votes to Provide Non-Lethal Options to Law Enforcement, Prevent Gun Violence

Foreign Policy.stance: Vindman’s foreign-policy views are strongly pro-Ukraine and focused on national security. He has said the U.S. turned its back on Ukraine and has argued that Russia’s war against Ukraine reflects a long-running threat to Ukrainian statehood and culture.

sources.title: Trump Whistleblower Alexander Vindman on Why the U.S. Has Turned Its Back on Ukraine
Trump repeats past US mistakes with Russia, Alexander Vindman says
Alexander Vindman | Foreign Affairs

Social Justice.stance: Vindman’s campaign messaging is centered on opposition to Trump, corruption, and democratic norms, but the sources reviewed did not provide a detailed, explicit social-justice platform. His public comments are more focused on accountability and public service than on a specific social-justice agenda.

sources.title: Alex Vindman, key Trump impeachment witness, launches Florida Senate run
Trump whistleblower Alex Vindman: 'Sitting on the sidelines was not ...'

Education.stance: Vindman has not released a detailed education policy platform in the sources reviewed. His campaign materials focus more on affordability, public service, and national security than on specific education proposals.

sources.title: Alex Vindman for Florida - Alex Vindman for Florida
Vindman's take on the special elections - POLITICO

Tech & AI.stance: Vindman has written that generative AI will significantly affect communication, learning, labor, and national security, and he argues Americans should understand both the limits and power of these tools. His emphasis is on AI as a national-security issue, especially how adversaries might use it against U.S. interests.

sources.title: On AI and National Security Work - by Alexander Vindman

Election Reform.stance: Vindman’s campaign is explicitly framed around defending democracy and checking corruption. The sources reviewed do not show a detailed election-reform bill agenda, but his public messaging strongly emphasizes democratic accountability and opposition to abuses of power.

sources.title: Alex Vindman for Florida - Alex Vindman for Florida
Alex Vindman, key figure in Trump's first impeachment, enters Florida Senate race

Local Issues.stance: Vindman says Florida families face high costs, high rents, high health-care premiums, and some of the nation’s highest property insurance rates. His campaign also ties his local message to disaster recovery, affordability, and opposition to what he describes as corruption and special interests.

sources.title: Alex Vindman for Florida - Alex Vindman for Florida
Alex Vindman, key figure in Trump's first impeachment, enters Florida Senate race

flags.concern: The description states this election is to fill 'Marco Rubio's term after his departure from the Senate' and frames Ashley Moody as the 'Republican incumbent.' This is internally inconsistent with the race date of 2026-11-03. If Rubio departed in 2025 (confirmed by Senate records), Ashley Moody would have been appointed to fill the vacancy. However, the characterization of the seat and why a special election is needed requires clarification about the actual vacancy and timeline. The description should explicitly state whether this is a special election to finish an unexpired term or a regular election cycle.
flags.suggestion: Clarify: (a) the exact date Rubio departed/why the seat became vacant, (b) whether Moody was appointed to fill the interim vacancy, (c) the precise term end date of the seat being contested, and (d) update the description to remove ambiguity about whether this is a special or regular election.

flags.concern: There are two entries for the exact same University of North Florida poll from 2026-03-02 with identical sample size (786) and overlapping matchups. The first entry lists both Moody vs. Jenkins and Moody vs. Vindman; the second lists only Moody vs. Vindman. This appears to be a duplicate or near-duplicate entry.
flags.suggestion: Consolidate the two polling entries into a single entry that includes all matchups from that date. Verify with the source whether this represents one poll or two separate polls.

flags.concern: Alex Vindman's voting_record array is empty ([]), yet there is a source URL 'https://vindman.house.gov/2026/02/12/vindman-votes-to-provide-non-lethal-options-to-law-enforcement-prevent-gun-violence/' cited in the 'Guns & Safety' issue section. This URL structure suggests a House member, but Vindman is not running for House office and has never served in Congress. This is a critical factual error.
flags.suggestion: Either: (a) remove the voting_record field if Vindman has no congressional voting record (appropriate for a non-incumbent), or (b) if the source is valid, add the actual voting record entry with correct details. Investigate whether this source URL is real or fabricated.

flags.concern: The top_donors array is empty for Vindman, despite him being a major candidate in a competitive race. For comparison, Moody has detailed donor information from OpenSecrets. This creates a significant asymmetry in candidate information and may reflect incomplete research.
flags.suggestion: Research and populate Vindman's top donors using FEC disclosures, OpenSecrets, or other campaign finance databases. If no donors have been publicly disclosed, note that explicitly with a source.

flags.concern: Vindman's education is accurately documented with three degrees from reputable institutions (Binghamton, Harvard, UT Austin). However, the profile lacks detail on his academic focus beyond field names. This is minor but inconsistent with the depth provided for Moody.
flags.suggestion: Optional: add brief context on relevant coursework or thesis topics, particularly for national security studies degree, to illustrate expertise alignment with foreign policy positions.

flags.concern: The stance reads: 'Vindman has not published a detailed abortion platform in the sources reviewed, but as a Democratic candidate he is generally aligned with protecting abortion access. This is an inference rather than a directly quoted candidate position.' This is explicitly an inference/assumption rather than candidate-stated policy, and the confidence is appropriately marked 'low.' However, it violates the principle of sourcing actual candidate positions.
flags.suggestion: Either locate a direct Vindman statement on abortion/reproductive rights, or reframe the stance to say 'No specific reproductive-rights platform statement found in campaign materials' without inferring party-line alignment.

flags.concern: Ashley Moody's LL.M. from Stetson University College of Law lists 'year: null'. The year field is missing. This is a minor data completeness issue.
flags.suggestion: Research and populate the year Moody completed her LL.M., or note that the completion year is not publicly available.

flags.concern: The summary sources for Moody are credible (official Senate website, campaign site, Congress.gov bioguide). However, the profile lacks news source citations in the summary_sources array despite drawing on news reporting elsewhere. This is minor but inconsistent.
flags.suggestion: Consider including a recent high-quality news profile (e.g., from AP, Reuters, or major state outlet) in summary_sources for added credibility.

flags.concern: Confidence is marked 'low' and the stance states: 'Vindman's public campaign materials reviewed here do not lay out a detailed climate platform.' This is accurate transparency about missing information, but the profile should attempt deeper research before settling for low-confidence placeholders.
flags.suggestion: Search for: (a) Vindman op-eds or published writing on climate/energy, (b) interviews where he discusses environmental policy, (c) any position statements on his campaign website. If nothing exists, consider stating 'no public climate platform' rather than providing a low-confidence inference.

flags.concern: The role 'Senior Fellow / Scholar' at 'Harvard University and related policy institutions' (2020–present) is vague. 'And related policy institutions' is imprecise. The end_year shows null, which is fine if ongoing, but the organization name should be more specific.
flags.suggestion: Specify which Harvard institute/center and name specific policy institutions where Vindman has held fellowships since 2020 (e.g., Kennedy School, Center for Strategic and International Studies, etc.).

flags.concern: Confidence is 'medium' and the stance acknowledges that Moody 'does not lay out a detailed foreign-policy platform.' This is fair, but the three sources cited are generic (campaign homepage, Senate homepage, Fox Business video link without title clarity). This represents a genuine gap in candidate transparency rather than a profile error, but it's worth noting.
flags.suggestion: No revision needed, but note in candidate summary that foreign policy is an area where Moody has not staked out detailed public positions beyond general alignment with Trump administration priorities.

reviews.summary: This profile demonstrates strong sourcing discipline and generally accurate candidate backgrounds. The Ashley Moody candidate profile is particularly well-documented with high-confidence issue stances backed by official sources and credible news outlets. However, there are several material issues that require revision: (1) a critical factual error in the race description about who won the seat, (2) significant asymmetry in Alex Vindman's profile—particularly missing donor data and a suspicious voting record entry citing a nonexistent congressional position, and (3) duplicate/redundant polling entries. These gaps and errors undermine confidence in the overall accuracy of the profile.

flags.concern: The candidate 'Jennifer Jenkins' is listed in this matchup but not referenced in the main description or other parts of the profile, potentially indicating an inconsistency or outdated information.
flags.suggestion: Verify the relevance of 'Jennifer Jenkins' based on current election data and either update the description to include her or remove her from the polling if she's not a primary contender.

flags.concern: The confidence level is marked as 'low' for Alex Vindman's stance, suggesting incomplete information on this issue based on the available sources.
flags.suggestion: Incorporate additional sources, such as recent campaign statements or policy documents, to provide a more detailed and confident assessment.

reviews.summary: This profile is a well-constructed and reliable resource, with thorough sourcing from credible outlets like official campaign websites, government databases such as Congress.gov and Bioguide, and established news sources like PBS and Florida Politics. It accurately captures candidate backgrounds, including detailed career histories and education for both Ashley Moody and Alex Vindman, while maintaining neutral language across issues and providing high-confidence stances where evidence is strong.
